description:
You are a QA engineer preparing a lightweight analysis of two test-suite logs that already exist on the system.  
    1. Location & initial data  
       • Directory: /home/user/qa_logs  
       • Pre-existing files:  
         a) /home/user/qa_logs/suite1.log  
         b) /home/user/qa_logs/suite2.log  
       Both logs follow *exactly* the same line format:  
         [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] TEST <test-name> ..... <STATUS>  
       where STATUS ∈ {PASS, FAIL, SKIP}.  
    2. Goal  
       Using nothing more than standard GNU text utilities (specifically sed and/or awk), create two “sanitized” log files that are stripped of their leading timestamp (the part enclosed in square brackets plus the trailing space) and then generate a concise CSV summary report that counts results per suite.  
    3. Required artefacts & precise formats  
       • Sanitized logs  
         - /home/user/qa_logs/suite1_clean.log  
         - /home/user/qa_logs/suite2_clean.log  
         Each line must keep exactly the substring starting with the literal word “TEST”; everything before “TEST” (including the brackets and the following space) must be removed. **Do not change the ordering of lines or alter white-space that appears after “TEST <test-name>”.**  
       • Summary report  
         - /home/user/qa_logs/summary_report.csv  
         The file must contain three lines in plain UTF-8, line-feed terminated (Unix LF), in the following strict format:  
           Line-1 (header):  
             suite,total,passed,failed,skipped  
           Line-2 (suite1 statistics):  
             suite1,<TOTAL1>,<PASSED1>,<FAILED1>,<SKIPPED1>  
           Line-3 (suite2 statistics):  
             suite2,<TOTAL2>,<PASSED2>,<FAILED2>,<SKIPPED2>  
         Replace the angle-bracketed placeholders with the numeric counts computed from the *sanitized* logs.  The order of columns and rows must match the example exactly; no additional spaces are allowed.  
    4. Constraints  
       • Solve the task using a maximum of four individual shell commands (pipelines are allowed).  
       • Do not move or rename any files except as specified above.  
       • Do not manually type the final numbers; they must be produced programmatically.  
    5. Verification hint  
       Automated grading will confirm:  
         – Existence of both *_clean.log files.  
         – That each *_clean.log line begins with the literal “TEST”.  
         – Correct numerical values and exact CSV formatting of /home/user/qa_logs/summary_report.csv.  
    When you are done, list (using e.g. cat) the contents of /home/user/qa_logs/summary_report.csv to show the final result.
truth:
Initial files and their exact contents (each line ends with LF):

    File: /home/user/qa_logs/suite1.log
    [2023-08-12 10:12:01] TEST test_login ..... PASS
    [2023-08-12 10:12:02] TEST test_logout ..... FAIL
    [2023-08-12 10:12:03] TEST test_signup ..... SKIP
    [2023-08-12 10:12:04] TEST test_profile_update ..... PASS
    [2023-08-12 10:12:05] TEST test_password_reset ..... PASS

    File: /home/user/qa_logs/suite2.log
    [2023-08-12 11:15:11] TEST api_fetch_user ..... PASS
    [2023-08-12 11:15:12] TEST api_create_user ..... FAIL
    [2023-08-12 11:15:13] TEST api_delete_user ..... FAIL
    [2023-08-12 11:15:14] TEST api_update_user ..... PASS

    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/qa_logs/suite1_clean.log
       TEST test_login ..... PASS
       TEST test_logout ..... FAIL
       TEST test_signup ..... SKIP
       TEST test_profile_update ..... PASS
       TEST test_password_reset ..... PASS

    2. /home/user/qa_logs/suite2_clean.log
       TEST api_fetch_user ..... PASS
       TEST api_create_user ..... FAIL
       TEST api_delete_user ..... FAIL
       TEST api_update_user ..... PASS

    3. /home/user/qa_logs/summary_report.csv
       suite,total,passed,failed,skipped
       suite1,5,3,1,1
       suite2,4,2,2,0
